summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the degree of f(x) = x^5 - 3x^3 + x - 7?

The degree is the highest power of x, which is 5.

What is the maximum number of real roots a degree-5 polynomial can have?

A polynomial of degree n can have at most n real roots. For degree 5, the maximum is 5.

What is the end behavior of f(x) = 2x^6 - x^3 + 1?

The leading term is 2x^6. Since the degree is even and the leading coefficient is positive, the graph rises on both ends.

What is the 'Rational Root Theorem'?

The Rational Root Theorem states that any rational root p/q of a polynomial with integer coefficients must have p dividing the constant term and q dividing the leading coefficient.

What is a 'higher-degree polynomial'?

Higher-degree polynomials typically refer to those of degree 3 (cubic) or higher, which can have more complex behavior than quadratics.

What is 'synthetic division'?

Synthetic division is an efficient algorithm for dividing a polynomial by (x - a) using only the coefficients, avoiding the full long division process.

What is 'polynomial long division'?

Polynomial long division systematically divides one polynomial by another, producing a quotient and remainder, analogous to integer long division.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: How many complex roots (counting multiplicity) does a degree-6 polynomial have?

By the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra, a degree-n polynomial has exactly n complex roots when counted with multiplicity.

Example: What does 'multiplicity' of a root mean?

If (x - r)^k is a factor of a polynomial, then r is a root with multiplicity k. Multiplicity affects whether the graph crosses or touches the x-axis at that root.

Example: A storage tank's volume function is V(x) = x^3 - 3x^2 - 4x + 12 cubic meters, where x is a dimension in meters. Factor this polynomial completely.

V(3) = 27 - 27 - 12 + 12 = 0. Dividing by (x - 3): x^2 - 4 = (x - 2)(x + 2). So V(x) = (x - 3)(x - 2)(x + 2).
